national_expectations.summary: Austria reach the knockout tie as heavy underdogs against European champions Spain, and Austrian media frame it accordingly: a side enjoying a historic, emotional high but expected to lose to a clear title favourite. Outlets stress that Austria arrive 'keineswegs chancenlos' (far from chanceless), leaning on a combative, compact and mentally resilient team. The dominant pre-match expectation is that simply reaching this stage already exceeds the baseline, so a win over Spain would be a 'Sensation' rather than the assumed outcome.

national_mood.summary: The national mood is euphoric and disbelieving after Sasa Kalajdzic's stoppage-time equaliser in a 3-3 thriller with Algeria flipped imminent elimination into qualification. Austrian and German-language press described it as 'voelliger WM-Wahnsinn' (complete World Cup madness), and ORF commentator Daniel Warmuth's on-air cry of 'Bist du deppert' was likened to Edi Finger's legendary 1978 outburst. Coverage frames the country as riding a wave of emotion and renewed self-belief into the Spain match.

manager_situation.summary: Ralf Rangnick, the German head coach of Austria, sits at a career-defining high after delivering the country's first World Cup knockout-round appearance in generations. He was visibly overwhelmed after the Algeria draw, saying he had never experienced anything like it in a football match. Before the decisive group game he had also publicly rejected any suggestion of engineering results to dodge Spain, insisting the team would always try to win. His standing is strong and unquestioned heading into the Spain tie, with coverage positioning him as the architect of Austria's pressing, transition-based identity.

historical_context.summary: Austria's run is historic: German-language reporting frames reaching the knockout round as their first since 1954 (when Austria finished third at the tournament in Switzerland) and their first advance past a World Cup group phase in 44 years, since 1982. The 3-3 with Algeria was repeatedly tied to two touchstones of Austrian football memory: the 1982 'Schande von Gijon' (Disgrace of Gijon) and Edi Finger's euphoric 1978 'I werd' narrisch' commentary from Cordoba.

dominant_storylines.storyline: The last-gasp miracle: Kalajdzic's stoppage-time header in a 3-3 draw with Algeria rescued Austria from elimination and sent them through as Group J runners-up, an ending described across the German-language press as 'complete World Cup madness' and one of the wildest games of the tournament.

Ending a generational drought: returning to the knockout round for the first time since 1954 and advancing past the group stage for the first time in 44 years frames Austria's campaign as historic, repeatedly measured against the ghosts of the 1982 'Disgrace of Gijon' and the 1978 Cordoba euphoria.

Underdog with a plan vs the European champions: previews cast Austria as compact, combative and mentally hardened under Rangnick, aiming to disrupt Spain through pressing and quick transitions, with experienced attackers Arnautovic, Sabitzer, Gregoritsch and Kalajdzic giving them a puncher's chance against a clear favourite.

No tanking to avoid Spain: ahead of the final group game, Austria publicly dismissed any idea of engineering a result to dodge the European champions, with Rangnick and Konrad Laimer insisting they would always try to win whatever the consequence for the bracket.

attributed_quotes.quote: I've never experienced anything like that in a football match before.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Das habe ich so noch nie erlebt.
attributed_quotes.context: Reacting after Kalajdzic's stoppage-time equaliser in the 3-3 draw with Algeria sent Austria into the knockout round.

attributed_quotes.quote: We go out, we want to win the game. It doesn't matter who we face.
attributed_quotes.context: Rejecting the idea that Austria would try to avoid a finishing position that meant facing Spain in the knockout round.

local_availability_reporting.detail: Posch suffered a jaw fracture against Jordan in the opener and returned wearing a special protective mask, playing in the subsequent group game against Argentina. Austrian reporting described his vision as unimpaired by the mask, but no confirmation specific to the Spain match was found.

gaps: No FIFA/UEFA/OEFB official-tier source was used; all mood, expectations, manager and historical context here are press-attributed (discovery) only.

The original draft's Rangnick quote was a non-verbatim conflation and was dropped; the replacement quote ('Das habe ich so noch nie erlebt') is the confirmed verbatim line, but no Rangnick or player quote referring directly to the Spain tactics/expectations was found.
Could not access the kicker.at preview and 'Sensation' survey pages directly (HTTP 403); their framing is captured via search summaries only.
No confirmed Austria probable XI or injury status specific to the Spain match was found; Posch is reported back with a protective mask but his Spain-match fitness is not separately confirmed.

Exact stadium unconfirmed at official level: recognized German outlets (weekend.at, t-online) place the tie at the Rose Bowl, Los Angeles, Thursday 2 July, 21:00 MESZ; the draft's 'SoFi Stadium' detail was not corroborated and was removed.

Conflicting historical color on the last Austria win over Spain: bundesliga.com frames a win as the first since 1990, while a kicker.at piece references no competitive win since 1978. The contested year was removed from prose and is flagged here.

An ESPN-aggregated snippet elsewhere loosely placed Austria in a different group with different goal tallies; this conflicts with the DB FACTS (Austria 2nd in Group J, 4 pts, W1 D1 L1) and German match reports, so the DB result was trusted and the snippet discarded. The ESPN article cited is dated 2026-06-26 and previews the Algeria game, so it is used only for the Laimer quote and Gijon context, not as a result source.

'erstmals seit 1954' knockout round; Spain next; Kalajdzic 3-3; LA Thursday 21:00 MESZ.
